name: py_script
description: Write and execute Python scripts from heredoc stdin, inline strings, or existing files. Automatically handles single-line and multi-line code. Replaces the common pattern of writing a .py file to /tmp, then running it with python3.
when_to_use: Use when you need to execute Python code that spans multiple lines (e.g., importing modules, defining functions, running experiments). Accepts heredoc input for complex scripts. Replaces the 'cat > /tmp/test_*.py << EOF ... EOF && python3 /tmp/test_*.py' boilerplate pattern. For simple one-liners, py_exec may be more convenient.

parameters.name: code_or_flag
parameters.description: Inline Python code as a string, '-f' to run an existing file, or leave empty to read code from stdin (piped/heredoc input).

parameters.name: script_file
parameters.description: When using '-f', the path to the Python script file to execute.

parameters.name: args
parameters.description: Additional arguments passed to the script when using '-f'.

examples.call: main.sh 'import sys; sys.path.insert(0, "/app"); from scapy.all import *; print("OK")"'

cat > /tmp/test.py << 'EOF' | main.sh
import os
print(os.getcwd())
EOF
examples.expected: Output of the Python script executed from heredoc input.
Output of executing the script file with arguments.

cost_saving_rationale: Replaces 2-4 separate bash commands (cat writing a script to /tmp, then python3 running it) with a single script call. Accepts heredoc input so multi-line scripts can be passed without shell escaping issues. Eliminates temp file management boilerplate.
